finding: Group ride availability is associated with lower utilisation in Local MaaS and multi-platform contexts within units
evidence: group_ride:local_maas_only is negative in 3/6 models; all unit FE = -0.052 (p = 0.0018), no-weak unit FE = -0.052 (p = 0.0018), scooter unit FE = -0.051 (p = 0.039). group_ride:multi_platform is also negative in 3/6 models; all unit FE = -0.087 (p < 0.001), no-weak unit FE = -0.087 (p < 0.001), scooter unit FE = -0.086 (p < 0.001).
interpretation: Group ride may mark a social/product affordance that is not aligned with platform-mediated utilisation, or it may proxy provider/city product positioning where utilisation is lower.
caveat: Market-FE estimates are unstable or not interpretable for this feature; keep as within-unit exploratory/moderate evidence.

finding: Vehicle-type breadth is positively associated with utilisation in scooter-only platform contexts
evidence: In scooter-only unit FE, no_of_vehicle_types_z interactions are positive for any platform (0.013, p < 0.001), local MaaS (0.023, p < 0.001), multi-platform (0.030, p < 0.001), and large aggregator (0.014, p < 0.001).
interpretation: Product assortment may help platform contexts by offering more modal fit within the same city-operator setting, especially in scooter-relevant markets.
caveat: Evidence is strongest only in scooter-only unit FE; market-FE and full-sample evidence is mixed.

finding: Complementary services show only small and partly artifact-prone effects
evidence: complementary_services:platform_any is negative in unit FE for all (-0.007, p = 0.008), no-weak (-0.012, p < 0.001), and scooter-only (-0.006, p = 0.031), but several platform-type terms are effectively zero with tiny p-values due to collinearity.
interpretation: Complementary services may be a small within-unit friction or positioning marker, but it is not yet a strong marketing-mix mechanism.
caveat: Do not use platform-type complementary-services coefficients that are near zero despite tiny p-values.

finding: Subscription_options is not reliable as currently coded
evidence: subscription_options equals 1 in 13,749 of 14,514 rows and 10,996 of 11,149 scooter rows. Some models produce implausibly huge unit-FE coefficients, indicating near-collinearity or weak support.
interpretation: The broad subscription_options dummy should not be used as a central feature; richer pass/free-unlock/minute-bundle coding is needed.
